name: UDID Card (Unique Disability ID)
description: The UDID Card initiative by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment provides a Unique Disability Identity Card to Persons with Disabilities (PwD) across India. It creates a standardized system for certification, data collection, and seamless delivery of benefits.
benefits: Single-point identification for accessing all government disability benefits
Elimination of duplicate or fake certificates
Seamless access to reservations, concessions, and welfare schemes at national level
Digital health and disability profiling
required_documents: Medical certificate from a government-recognized doctor/hospital
Proof of residence (Ration Card / Voter ID / Utility Bill)

name: ADIP Scheme (Assistive Devices for PwD)
description: The Assistance to Disabled Persons (ADIP) Scheme is funded by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment. It assists PwDs in procuring durable, sophisticated, and scientifically manufactured modern, standard aids and assistive devices that can promote their physical, social, and psychological rehabilitation.
benefits: Free or subsidized assistive devices (wheelchairs, hearing aids, crutches, Braille kits, etc.)
Devices provided through camps organized by NGOs/government hospitals
Battery-operated wheelchairs for persons with severe locomotor disability
Support for fitting of artificial limbs
required_documents: UDID Card or disability certificate from a recognized medical authority
Income certificate issued by competent authority
Prescription for the required assistive device from a medical officer

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
description: A scholarship scheme by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD) to support students with disabilities studying in Classes 9 and 10. It aims to reduce the dropout rate among students with disabilities and encourage continuation of education.
benefits: Scholarship of ₹700/month for day scholars and ₹1100/month for hostellers
Additional book grant of ₹1000 per annum
Ad-hoc grant of ₹2000 per annum for incidental expenses
Assistance with examination fee reimbursement
required_documents: Income certificate of parent/guardian (annual income not exceeding ₹2.5 lakh)
Aadhaar Card of student and parent
Bank account details (in student's name or joint with parent)

name: Niramaya Health Insurance Scheme
description: Niramaya is a health insurance scheme specifically designed for persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation, and Multiple Disabilities. It is implemented by the National Trust under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment in collaboration with insurance companies to provide health coverage to persons with these specific disabilities.
benefits: Health insurance cover up to ₹1 lakh per annum
OPD coverage up to ₹15,000 per year
Annual premium of only ₹500 (BPL families: ₹250)
Coverage for hospitalization, surgeries, and alternative therapies (Ayurveda, Yoga, etc.)
Coverage for transportation charges for medical visits
required_documents: Disability certificate from a recognized medical board
BPL card (if applicable) for concessional premium
Guardian's ID proof (if beneficiary is a minor or dependent)

name: National Fellowship for Persons with Disabilities
description: The National Fellowship for Persons with Disabilities (NFPwD), administered by the University Grants Commission (UGC) and funded by DEPwD, provides financial assistance to PwD students pursuing higher education (M.Phil. & Ph.D.). It aims to increase research opportunities and promote inclusion in academia.
benefits: Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) of ₹31,000/month for the first two years
Senior Research Fellowship (SRF) of ₹35,000/month for the remaining tenure
HRA as per university norms
Annual contingency grant of ₹10,000 (JRF) and ₹20,500 (SRF)
200 slots available per year across all disciplines
required_documents: Disability certificate with minimum 40% benchmark disability
Proof of admission to M.Phil./Ph.D. program
